# n-dx

> Codebase analysis generated by Sourcevision

## Project Overview

- **Files**: 349
- **Lines of code**: 89,649
- **Languages**: TypeScript (279), JSON (22), CSS (16), Other (10), Markdown (10)
- **Analyzed**: 2026-02-05T14:03:09.135Z

## Architecture Zones

### Hench Agent Core (`hench-core`)

Main autonomous agent implementation including the tool-use loop, prompt construction, and run management.

- Files: 46
- Cohesion: 0.94, Coupling: 0.06
- Entry points: packages/hench/src/schema/index.ts, packages/hench/src/schema/v1.ts, packages/hench/src/types/index.ts
- Detail: `.sourcevision/zones/hench-core/context.md`

### Hench Integration Bridge (`hench-integration`)

Completion validation and Rex tool integration that bridges the agent with external systems.

- Files: 5
- Cohesion: 0.67, Coupling: 0.33
- Entry points: packages/hench/src/agent/completion.ts, packages/hench/src/tools/rex.ts
- Detail: `.sourcevision/zones/hench-integration/context.md`

### Hench Tool Infrastructure (`hench-tooling`)

Tool definitions, shell execution, and security guard system for safe autonomous operations.

- Files: 12
- Cohesion: 0.80, Coupling: 0.20
- Entry points: packages/hench/src/agent/tools.ts, packages/hench/src/guard/index.ts
- Detail: `.sourcevision/zones/hench-tooling/context.md`

### Orchestration Layer (`orchestration`)

Top-level CLI entry points and configuration that coordinate the three sub-packages.

- Files: 3
- Cohesion: 1.00, Coupling: 0.00
- Detail: `.sourcevision/zones/orchestration/context.md`

### Rex PRD Management CLI (`packages-rex:rex-cli`)

A self-contained CLI tool and MCP server for managing hierarchical product requirements documents, including code analysis, tree operations, validation, persistence, and AI-agent workflow integration.

- Files: 36
- Cohesion: 1.00, Coupling: 0.00
- Detail: `.sourcevision/zones/packages-rex:rex-cli/context.md`

### Analysis Engine (`packages-sourcevision:analysis-engine`)

Core analysis pipeline including file inventory, import graph extraction, Louvain community detection, route parsing, AI enrichment, and CLI orchestration.

- Files: 33
- Cohesion: 0.94, Coupling: 0.06
- Entry points: packages/sourcevision/src/analyzers/components.ts, packages/sourcevision/src/analyzers/imports.ts, packages/sourcevision/src/analyzers/inventory.ts, packages/sourcevision/src/analyzers/zones.ts, packages/sourcevision/src/schema/data-files.ts
- Detail: `.sourcevision/zones/packages-sourcevision:analysis-engine/context.md`

### Interactive Viewer (`packages-sourcevision:interactive-viewer`)

Preact single-page application that visualizes analysis results through force-directed graphs, zone maps, route trees, and findings panels, consuming only schema types with zero analyzer dependencies.

- Files: 46
- Cohesion: 0.96, Coupling: 0.04
- Entry points: packages/sourcevision/src/schema/v1.ts
- Detail: `.sourcevision/zones/packages-sourcevision:interactive-viewer/context.md`

### Test Fixtures (`packages-sourcevision:test-fixtures`)

Self-contained sample TypeScript project used as input data for integration and end-to-end tests, intentionally isolated with no imports from or exports to production code.

- Files: 7
- Cohesion: 1.00, Coupling: 0.00
- Detail: `.sourcevision/zones/packages-sourcevision:test-fixtures/context.md`

### Test Suite (`packages-sourcevision:test-suite`)

Unit, integration, and end-to-end tests covering analyzer logic, schema validation, CLI workflows, and the serve command, with imports reaching into both analyzer and viewer zones.

- Files: 5
- Cohesion: 0.46, Coupling: 0.54
- Entry points: packages/sourcevision/src/schema/validate.ts
- Detail: `.sourcevision/zones/packages-sourcevision:test-suite/context.md`

## Key Dependencies

- `packages/rex/src/core/tree.ts` (26 imports)
- `packages/rex/src/store/index.ts` (22 imports)

- `packages/rex/src/cli/output.ts` (19 imports)

- `packages/rex/src/cli/errors.ts` (18 imports)
- `packages/hench/src/schema/v1.ts` (15 imports)
- `packages/rex/src/core/canonical.ts` (14 imports)

- `preact` (used by 25 files)
- `rex` (used by 10 files)
- `zod` (used by 7 files)
- `@anthropic-ai/sdk` (used by 3 files)
- `typescript` (used by 3 files)
- `@modelcontextprotocol/sdk` (used by 2 files)
- `child_process` (used by 2 files)
- `path` (used by 2 files)
- `url` (used by 2 files)

## Route Structure

### Route Tree

```
/ → packages/sourcevision/tests/fixtures/remix-app/app/routes/_index.tsx
/users/:id → packages/sourcevision/tests/fixtures/remix-app/app/routes/users.$id.tsx
```

### Convention Exports

- action: 2 modules
- default: 4 modules
- loader: 2 modules
- meta: 1 modules

### Components: 5 total, 0 usage edges

## Findings

### Anti-patterns

- Hench depends on Rex at runtime but no workspace dependency declared in package.json — implicit coupling may break with package boundary changes [critical]
- Test files (.test.ts) within hench-core and hench-tooling zones inflate cohesion metrics — consider whether test co-location masks actual coupling issues [info]
- Token budget and prompt modules are tightly coupled to agent loop but have no clear interface boundary — changes to prompting strategy require touching multiple files [info] (hench-core)
- Zone contains 46 files spanning agent logic, CLI, runs, storage, and utilities — violates single responsibility; recommend splitting into agent/, cli/, and storage/ zones [warning] (hench-core)
- rex.ts tool directly imports Rex types without a stable contract — Rex schema changes will break hench without compile-time guarantees [warning] (hench-integration)
- Guard modules (commands.ts, paths.ts) duplicate allowlist patterns that could be consolidated into a single declarative config [info] (hench-tooling)
- tools.ts re-exports individual tool modules but also contains orchestration logic — split into tools/index.ts (exports only) and tools/registry.ts (orchestration) [warning] (hench-tooling)

### Patterns

- No sourcevision or rex packages detected in analysis — either excluded from scan or in separate workspaces [info]
- Agent loop (loop.ts, cli-loop.ts) forms the spine that other zones attach to via well-defined interfaces [info] (hench-core)
- Integration zone is thin (5 files) and focused — good separation of concerns for external tool coupling [info] (hench-integration)
- Tools (files, git, shell) share guard infrastructure but remain individually testable [info] (hench-tooling)

### Relationships

- Bidirectional hench-core ↔ hench-tooling coupling (4+5 imports) suggests shared types should be extracted to a dedicated types module [warning]
- Core zone accepts 9 inbound imports but only reaches out to tooling (4) — appropriate dependency direction [info] (hench-core)
- Rex tool wrapper isolates PRD protocol details from core agent logic [info] (hench-integration)
- Tooling imports from core (5) for shared types but core also imports tooling (4) — bidirectional coupling creates potential for circular dependency issues [warning] (hench-tooling)
- Orchestration zone has zero inbound dependencies — acts as pure entry point with no reverse coupling [info] (orchestration)

### Observations

- Bidirectional coupling: "hench-core" ↔ "hench-tooling" (4+5 crossings) — consider extracting shared interface [warning]
- Size imbalance: largest zone has 46 files vs smallest with 3 — uneven decomposition [info]
- Contains 51% of project files (46/91) — may be too broad, consider splitting [warning] (hench-core)
- High cohesion (0.94) — files are tightly interconnected [info] (hench-core)
- High cohesion (0.8) — files are tightly interconnected [info] (hench-tooling)
- High cohesion (1) — files are tightly interconnected [info] (orchestration)
- All four zones maintain cohesion above 0.67, indicating healthy module boundaries [info]
- Bidirectional imports between hench-core and hench-integration (4 each way via hench zones) may indicate opportunity to clarify API boundaries [warning]
- Orchestration layer has zero coupling which validates the delegation architecture [info]
- 46 files with 0.94 cohesion demonstrates strong modular design within the agent package [info] (hench-core)
- ... and 10 more

### Suggestions

- Add shared error types package or module to formalize error contracts between zones [info]
- Document the implicit contract that hench-integration serves as the only mutation path to Rex PRD [info]
- Implement run history retention policy to prevent .hench/runs/ unbounded growth [warning]
- agent/prompt.ts and agent/brief.ts both construct LLM prompts — consider consolidating prompt-building logic to reduce duplication [info] (hench-core)
- stuck.ts detection logic could benefit from explicit state enum rather than heuristic string matching for robustness [info] (hench-core)
- Merge completion.test.ts and completion-validation.test.ts or clarify distinct responsibilities in naming [info] (hench-integration)
- Standardize guard modules to use consistent allowlist or denylist pattern for both commands and paths [warning] (hench-tooling)
- tools/test-runner.ts was recently moved from agent/ — verify all imports updated and no stale references remain [info] (hench-tooling)
